response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates impressive technical breadth and seniority across multiple domains, they lack critical requirements for this C++ specialist role. Their C++ experience is outdated (2014–2015), and their recent 10+ years have focused entirely on web/SaaS development with JavaScript/TypeScript. The role requires hands-on C++ architecture, embedded systems expertise, industrial automation domain knowledge, and IEC 62443 security compliance—none of which are evident in the resume. This represents a significant career pivot without prior IoT or industrial systems background.
strengths: Strong software engineering fundamentals—demonstrates TDD, CI/CD, code quality focus, and software craftsmanship across multiple roles
Proven mentorship and leadership—organized workshops, gave technical talks, contributed to open-source projects, mentored team members
Full-stack capability and rapid technology adoption—successfully transitioned across web, mobile, desktop, and multiple programming languages
concerns: Critical C++ experience gap—last substantive C++ work was 2014–2015; entire recent career is JavaScript/TypeScript and web development
No industrial automation or IoT background—all experience centers on web, SaaS, and data platforms with zero embedded systems exposure
Missing security and compliance expertise—no evidence of SDL practices, IEC 62443 standards, or secure coding for industrial/critical systems

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or embedded systems experience
Strong general software engineering and mentorship track record
